root_cause.summary: Two independent extractors produce the @exports symbol list with different traversal logic. --source-headers writes @exports using element.exports from buildSemanticElementsFromState (semantic/header-generator.ts:118), which aggregates element-level exports collected by the element extractor. The staleness checker at orchestrator.ts:473-480 uses rawExports from extractRawExports, a separate tree-sitter traversal that walks export declarations. The two paths produce different symbol sets for the same file, causing headerStatus='stale' for files whose @exports header is actually correct.
divergence_examples: ConversionOptions — exported as a type alias; element extractor may see it, rawExports may emit it under a different name form
convertGraphToElements, getConversionStats — function exports; one path may include re-exports the other misses
root_cause.fix_approach: Normalize astSet in the staleness check to exclude symbols that rawExports captures but element.exports does not: type-only exports, re-exports, and namespace re-exports. Alternatively: derive the astSet from the same element.exports path the header-generator uses, bypassing rawExports entirely for the staleness check.

risks.title: Masking genuine staleness
risks.mitigation: Add a test with a file whose @exports header IS genuinely stale and verify headerStatus=stale is still emitted after the fix.

risks.title: Build breakage from type change
risks.mitigation: Run npm run build after the edit; TypeScript will catch any type errors immediately.

candidates.change: Replace astSet construction from rawExports with element.exports-derived set (same source header-generator uses), OR add normalization to strip type-only and re-exported names from astSet before comparison
Add test: file with @exports matching element.exports but not rawExports → must yield headerStatus=current after fix

decision_records.decision: Use element.exports as the astSet source instead of rawExports for the staleness check
decision_records.rationale: This ensures both sides of the comparison (header @exports and astSet) come from the same extractor, eliminating the divergence by construction. rawExports serves import-resolution purposes and should not gate header staleness.
